macros.token:
What it does ============
PICRUSt2 (Phylogenetic Investigation of Communities by Reconstruction of Unobserved States) is a tool for predicting functional abundances based only on marker gene sequences.
Read more about the tool: https://github.com/picrust/picrust2/wiki

## determine project dir which is something like /lib/python3.8/site-packages/picrust2/default_files/ PROJECT_DIR=\$(python -c 'from picrust2 import default; print(default.project_dir)') && REF_DIR_BASE=\$PROJECT_DIR"/default_files/" && #if $ref_dir.selector == "custom" mkdir -p custom/ && ln -s '$ref_dir.custom_fna' custom/custom.fna && ln -s '$ref_dir.custom_hmm' custom/custom.hmm && #if $placement_tool == "epa-ng" ln -s '$ref_dir.custom_model' custom/custom.model && #else ln -s '$ref_dir.custom_model' custom/custom.raxml_info && #end if ln -s '$ref_dir.custom_tre' custom/custom.tre && #end if

#if $place_seqs_section.ref_dir1.selector == "custom" mkdir -p custom_ref1/ && ln -s '$place_seqs_section.ref_dir1.custom_fna' custom_ref1/custom_ref1.fna && ln -s '$place_seqs_section.ref_dir1.custom_hmm' custom_ref1/custom_ref1.hmm && #if $place_seqs_section.placement_tool == "epa-ng" ln -s '$place_seqs_section.ref_dir1.custom_model' custom_ref1/custom_ref1.model && #else ln -s '$place_seqs_section.ref_dir1.custom_model' custom_ref1/custom_ref1.raxml_info && #end if ln -s '$place_seqs_section.ref_dir1.custom_tre' custom_ref1/custom_ref1.tre && #end if #if $place_seqs_section.ref_dir2.selector == "custom" mkdir -p custom_ref2/ && ln -s '$place_seqs_section.ref_dir2.custom_fna' custom_ref2/custom_ref2.fna && ln -s '$place_seqs_section.ref_dir2.custom_hmm' custom_ref2/custom_ref2.hmm && #if $place_seqs_section.placement_tool == "epa-ng" ln -s '$place_seqs_section.ref_dir2.custom_model' custom_ref2/custom_ref2.model && #else ln -s '$place_seqs_section.ref_dir2.custom_model' custom_ref2/custom_ref2.raxml_info && #end if ln -s '$place_seqs_section.ref_dir2.custom_tre' custom_ref2/custom_ref2.tre && #end if

#if $hsp_section.trait_input.selector == "custom" mkdir -p custom_traits_ref1/ custom_traits_ref2/ && #set $custom_trait_tables_ref1 = [] #for $i, $trait_table in enumerate($hsp_section.trait_input.custom_trait_tables_ref1) #set $trait_path = "custom_traits_ref1/custom_traits_%d.tsv" % $i #silent $custom_trait_tables_ref1.append($trait_path) ln -s '$trait_table' '$trait_path' && #end for #set $custom_trait_tables_ref1_arg = ",".join($custom_trait_tables_ref1) #set $custom_trait_tables_ref2 = [] #for $i, $trait_table in enumerate($hsp_section.trait_input.custom_trait_tables_ref2) #set $trait_path = "custom_traits_ref2/custom_traits_%d.tsv" % $i #silent $custom_trait_tables_ref2.append($trait_path) ln -s '$trait_table' '$trait_path' && #end for #set $custom_trait_tables_ref2_arg = ",".join($custom_trait_tables_ref2) #if $hsp_section.trait_input.marker_gene_table_ref1 ln -s '$hsp_section.trait_input.marker_gene_table_ref1' custom_marker_ref1.tsv && #end if #if $hsp_section.trait_input.marker_gene_table_ref2 ln -s '$hsp_section.trait_input.marker_gene_table_ref2' custom_marker_ref2.tsv && #end if #end if

param.label: Study sequences
param.help: Sequences of the representative OTUs and/or ASVs. Sequences need to be on the positive strand and the headerline should be only one field, i.e. no additional whitespace-delimited fields

param.label: Placement tool
param.help: Used for placing sequences into reference tree
param.option: EPA-ng - Fast, parallel, highly accurate Maximum Likelihood Phylogenetic Placement, by the team behind RAxML(-ng)

param.name: selector
param.label: Reference data
param.help: Used for sequence placement

param.name: custom_hmm
param.label: Hidden-markov model of the multiple-sequence alignment
param.help: The HMM of the alignment can be created using hmmbuild

param.name: custom_model
param.label: Modelfile
param.help: For epa-ng: output by RaXmL specifying the best parameters for the tree, for sepp see examples in PICRUSt2 repository

param.label: Minimum alignment length
param.help: Proportion of the total length of an input query sequence that must align with reference sequences. Sequences with lengths below this value will be excluded from the placement and all subsequent steps

param.label: Study sequences
param.help: Sequences of the representative OTUs and/or ASVs. Sequences need to be on the positive strand and the headerline should be only one field, i.e. no additional whitespace-delimited fields

param.label: Placement tool
param.help: Used for placing sequences into reference tree
param.option: EPA-ng - Fast, parallel, highly accurate Maximum Likelihood Phylogenetic Placement, by the team behind RAxML(-ng)

param.name: selector
param.label: Domain 1 reference data
param.help: Bacterial reference data are used by default

param.name: custom_hmm
param.label: Hidden-markov model of the multiple-sequence alignment
param.help: The HMM of the alignment can be created using hmmbuild

param.name: custom_model
param.label: Modelfile
param.help: For epa-ng: output by RaXmL specifying the best parameters for the tree, for sepp see examples in PICRUSt2 repository

param.name: selector
param.label: Domain 2 reference data
param.help: Archaeal reference data are used by default

param.name: custom_hmm
param.label: Hidden-markov model of the multiple-sequence alignment
param.help: The HMM of the alignment can be created using hmmbuild

param.name: custom_model
param.label: Modelfile
param.help: For epa-ng: output by RaXmL specifying the best parameters for the tree, for sepp see examples in PICRUSt2 repository

param.label: Minimum alignment length
param.help: Proportion of the total length of an input query sequence that must align with reference sequences. Sequences with lengths below this value will be excluded from the placement and all subsequent steps

data.name: out_tree
data.label: ${tool.name} on ${on_string}: Tree of reference and study 16S sequences @LABEL_SUFFIX@

macros.token:
#if $hsp_section.trait_input.selector == "default" --in_traits '$hsp_section.trait_input.in_traits' #else if $hsp_section.trait_input.selector == "custom" --custom_trait_tables_ref1 '$custom_trait_tables_ref1_arg' --custom_trait_tables_ref2 '$custom_trait_tables_ref2_arg' #if $hsp_section.trait_input.marker_gene_table_ref1 --marker_gene_table_ref1 custom_marker_ref1.tsv #end if #if $hsp_section.trait_input.marker_gene_table_ref2 --marker_gene_table_ref2 custom_marker_ref2.tsv #end if #end if
--hsp_method '$hsp_section.hsp_method_options.hsp_method' #if $hsp_section.hsp_method_options.hsp_method == "mp" --edge_exponent $hsp_section.hsp_method_options.edge_exponent #end if

param.name: selector
param.label: Trait table@IN_TRAIT_LABEL_SUFF@
param.help: i.e. which gene families to predict

param.label: Hidden-state prediction method
param.option: Predict discrete traits by: Maximum parsimony (mp)
Predict discrete traits by: Empirical state probabilities across tips (emp_prob)
Predict continuous traits by: Subtree averaging (subtree_average)
Predict continuous traits by: phylogentic independent contrast (pic)
Reconstruct continuous traits by: squared-change parsimony (scp)

param.label: Transition cost weight
param.help: Specifies weighting transition costs by the inverse length of edge lengths. If 0, then edge lengths do not influence predictions

param.name: selector
param.label: Trait tables
param.help: i.e. which gene families to predict

param.label: Pre-calculated trait tables
param.help: Note that EC numbers will always be predicted unless --no_pathways is set

param.label: Customized trait table(s) for domain 1
param.help: with gene families as columns and genomes as rows

param.label: Customized trait table(s) for domain 2
param.help: with gene families as columns and genomes as rows

param.label: Table of predicted marker gene (16S or other) copy numbers for domain 1
Table of predicted marker gene (16S or other) copy numbers for domain 2
Hidden-state prediction method
param.option: Predict discrete traits by: Maximum parsimony (mp)
Predict discrete traits by: Empirical state probabilities across tips (emp_prob)
Predict continuous traits by: Subtree averaging (subtree_average)
Predict continuous traits by: phylogentic independent contrast (pic)
Reconstruct continuous traits by: squared-change parsimony (scp)

param.label: Transition cost weight
param.help: Specifies weighting transition costs by the inverse length of edge lengths. If 0, then edge lengths do not influence predictions

param.label: Default trait database
param.help: Use SC for the current PICRUSt2 default database or oldIMG for legacy IMG default trait tables

param.label: Reference domain
param.help: Used with the SC default trait database

macros.token: #set $_input=$input #if $_input.ext == "mothur.shared" #set ext="msf" #else if $_input.ext == "tabular" #set ext="tsv" #else if $_input.ext.startswith('biom') #set ext="biom" #else >&2 "unknown extension $_input.ext" exit 1; #end if ln -s '$input' 'input.$ext' &&

#set $_input=$metagenome_pipeline_section.input #if $_input.ext == "mothur.shared" #set ext="msf" #else if $_input.ext == "tabular" #set ext="tsv" #else if $_input.ext.startswith('biom') #set ext="biom" #else >&2 "unknown extension $_input.ext" exit 1; #end if ln -s '$metagenome_pipeline_section.input' 'input.$ext' &&

param.label: Sequence abundance table (OTUs or ASVs)
param.help: The sequence abundances should be in read counts and not relative abundances. The tool will normalize the input sequence abundance table by the predicted number of marker genes

param.label: Minimum number of reads across all samples for each input ASV
param.help: ASVs below this cut-off will be counted as part of the RARE category in the stratified output

param.label: Minimum number of samples that an ASV needs to be identfied within
param.help: ASVs below this cut-off will be counted as part of the RARE category in the stratified output

param.label: Maximum Nearest-sequenced taxon index (NSTI)
param.help: Sequences with larger values will be excluded

param.name: selector
param.label: Generate an output table stratified by sequences
Output wide-format stratified table of metagenome predictions
param.help: This is the deprecated method of generating stratified tables since it is extremely memory intensive

param.label: Skip normalizing sequence abundances by predicted marker gene copy numbers
Customized table mapping of pathways to reactions
param.help: Default mapping file is Maps MetaCyc reactions to prokaryotic MetaCyc pathways

param.label: Run MinPath to identify which pathways are present as a first pass
Perform gap filling before predicting pathway abundances
Regroup input gene families to reactions
Mapfile of ids to regroup gene families to before running MinPath
param.help: Keep empty to use the default mapping file (ec_level4_to_metacyc_rxn.tsv contained in PICRUSt2)

param.label: Calculate pathway abundances for each individual predicted genome
param.help: The output will be the predicted pathway abundance contributed by each individual sequence. This is in contrast to the default stratified output, which is the contribution to the community-wide pathway abundances. Note this will greatly increase the runtime. Experimental pathway coverage stratified by contributing sequence will also be output when --coverage is set

param.label: Table of sequence abundances across samples normalized by marker copy number
param.help: Typically the normalized sequence abundance table output at the metagenome pipeline step. This input is required when the per sequence contrib option is set

param.label: Table of function abundances per sequence, which was outputted at the hidden-state prediction step
param.help: This input is required when the per sequence contrib option is set. Note that this file should be the same input table as used for the metagenome pipeline step

param.label: Output wide-format stratified table (DEPRECATED)
param.help: Instead of the metagenome contribution table. This is the deprecated method of generating stratified tables since it is extremely memory intensive

param.label: Calculate pathway coverages as well as abundances
param.help: Experimental and only useful for advanced users

data.name: path_abun_unstrat_per_seq
data.label: ${tool.name} on ${on_string}: Pathway abundance unstratified per sequence @LABEL_SUFFIX@
